summary: Final frontend retest completed on public URL for graph drag reliability and sorting playback/comparison flow. Graph add-node, edge-mode creation, BFS run, and playback availability worked. Sorting comparison mode remains present and playback becomes enabled after run completion. Graph node drag is still inconsistent from both label and circle interaction areas under repeated attempts.

ui_bugs.issue: Drag initiation/movement remains unreliable in repeated trials from both label and circle areas. Latest matrix: label [true,false,true], circle [false,false,false].

action_items: Fix GraphCanvas drag lifecycle so drag-start from node hit area always leads to deterministic movement (label and circle both reliable).
Add a deterministic UI regression check asserting node position changes across repeated drag attempts from both hit targets.
After drag fix, re-run graph smoke (add node, edge mode, BFS) to ensure no regressions.
critical_code_review_comments: GraphCanvas relies on SVG-level onMouseMove + per-node onMouseDown state toggles; current event sequencing still behaves non-deterministically under repeated pointer drags.
Sorting flow state transition is now correct for requested scenario: playback is enabled once run completes and comparison UI remains available.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Public URL tested: https://visualgo-pro.preview.emergentagent.com. Verified PASS: Graph page loads, node creation works, edge mode works, BFS run completes with playback enabled; Sorting comparison toggle/config visible and playback button enabled after successful completion. Verified FAIL: drag reliability still inconsistent in repeated matrix checks (label and circle not consistently moving node).
rca of the issue: Repro: open /graph, add nodes, then repeatedly drag same node from label center and circle center using identical pointer deltas. Observed intermittent non-movement despite valid drag distances. Likely root cause remains drag event lifecycle inconsistency between node-level drag start and SVG-level move/up handling, causing occasional lost drag state or missed move propagation.
